_metadata.source: Defense Health Agency (DHA), TRICARE Health Plan
_metadata.notes: CY 2026 TRICARE costs. Group A = sponsor entered service before Jan 1, 2018. Group B = on/after Jan 1, 2018. Pharmacy costs added in v2026.2.

group_definitions.group_a: Sponsor's initial enlistment or appointment began BEFORE January 1, 2018
group_definitions.group_b: Sponsor's initial enlistment or appointment began ON OR AFTER January 1, 2018

retiree_costs._note: Costs for retirees, their family members, survivors, and others

enrollment_fee_medically_retired._note: Frozen at rate when classified; see medically_retired_fee_schedule

copays_network._hospitalization_note: $250/day or up to 25% of hospital charges, whichever is less, plus 20% of separately billed services

deductible_annual._note: Prescription costs also apply to deductible

active_duty_family_costs._note: Active duty service members have $0 out-of-pocket. Costs below are for their family members and transitional survivors.

pharmacy_costs._note: CY 2026-2027 TRICARE Pharmacy Program costs. NDAA FY2018 Sec 702 established copay schedule through Dec 31, 2027. Copays vary by formulary status, pharmacy type, and beneficiary category.

formulary_categories.generic_formulary: Lowest cost tier. FDA-approved generic equivalent of brand-name drug on TRICARE formulary.
formulary_categories.non_formulary: Drug not on TRICARE formulary. Provider must establish medical necessity for coverage at formulary copay. Otherwise full non-formulary copay applies.
formulary_categories.non_covered: Drug not covered by TRICARE. Beneficiary pays full cost.

pharmacy_types.military: Military hospital or clinic pharmacy. Up to 90-day supply.
pharmacy_types.home_delivery: TRICARE Pharmacy Home Delivery (Express Scripts). Up to 90-day supply.
pharmacy_types.retail_network: TRICARE retail network pharmacy. Up to 30-day supply.
pharmacy_types.non_network_domestic: Non-network retail pharmacy in US and US territories.
pharmacy_types.non_network_overseas: Non-network retail pharmacy outside US and US territories.

active_duty_service_members._note: ADSMs pay $0 for covered drugs at military pharmacies, home delivery, and retail network pharmacies. Non-formulary drugs require medical necessity. Non-network: pay full cost up front, file claim for reimbursement.

active_duty_family_prime_remote_us._note: AD family members enrolled in TRICARE Prime Remote in the US. Effective Feb 28, 2026, $0 copays for covered drugs at military, home delivery, and retail network pharmacies. Non-formulary requires medical necessity.

standard_beneficiaries._note: All beneficiaries EXCEPT medically retired sponsors/dependents and survivors of ADSMs. Includes retirees, retiree family members, reserve/guard members, and dependents. NDAA FY2018 rates effective CY 2026-2027.

military.non_formulary_note: Generally not available without medical necessity

prime_enrollees._note: 50% cost-share applies after meeting point-of-service annual deductible

all_other_beneficiaries._note: $48 or 20% of total cost, whichever is more, after annual deductible
all_other_beneficiaries._note_nf: $85 or 20% of total cost, whichever is more, after annual deductible

adsm_adfm_prime_overseas._note: May have to pay full cost up front and file claim for reimbursement

medically_retired_and_adsm_survivors._note: NDAA FY2018 Sec 702 froze pharmacy copays at 2017 rates for: (1) medically retired service members and their family members, (2) survivors of active duty service members. These rates do NOT change with standard copay increases.

military.non_formulary_note: Generally not available without medical necessity

prime_enrollees._note: 50% cost-share after POS deductible ($300 individual / $600 family)

select_enrollees._note: $24 or 20% of total cost, whichever is more, after annual deductible

prior_year_copays_2025._note: CY 2024-2025 standard beneficiary copays for year-over-year comparison. NDAA FY2018 rates. Medically retired/survivor rates unchanged.

yoy_change_2025_to_2026._note: 12% to 15.8% increase over 2024-2025 rates per Federal Register notice

pharmacy_costs.non_covered_drugs: Beneficiary pays full cost. Not available at military pharmacies or home delivery.

premium_based_plans._note: Monthly premiums for premium-based TRICARE plans. These are separate from Prime/Select enrollment fees.

chcbp_quarterly._note: Continued Health Care Benefit Program; quarterly premiums

tricare_for_life._note: For Medicare-eligible beneficiaries with TRICARE. Must have Medicare Parts A and B.

deductible_annual._note: Only when TRICARE is the only payer

cost_sharing.services_covered_by_both: Medicare pays approved amount, TRICARE pays remainder, you pay $0
cost_sharing.services_covered_by_medicare_only: You pay Medicare deductible and cost-share
cost_sharing.services_covered_by_tricare_only: You pay TRICARE deductible and cost-share

hospital_stay.days_151_plus: TRICARE is primary payer; you pay TRICARE cost-share

skilled_nursing.days_101_plus: TRICARE is primary payer with pre-authorization

medically_retired_fee_schedule._note: TRICARE Prime enrollment fee freezes at the rate when classified in DEERS as medically retired or survivor. Frozen as long as no break in Prime enrollment.

open_season.applies_to: TRICARE Prime and TRICARE Select plan changes
open_season._note: Changes effective January 1 of following year
